parsedContent.title: CERTIFIED ACCOUNTING TECHNICIAN LEVEL 1 EXAMINATION L1.1: INTRODUCTION TO FINANCIAL ACCOUNTING
parsedContent.description: This exam consists of two sections: A and B. Section A has one compulsory question, while Section B has four questions, with three to be attempted.

sections.name: A
sections.title: SECTION A
sections.description: This section has one compulsory question

questions.text: Explain the purpose of the following financial statements: i) Statement of financial position (2 marks) ii) Income statement (2 marks)

subQuestions.label: i)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: To provide a snapshot of a company's financial position at a specific point in time

subQuestions.label: ii)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: To show a company's revenues and expenses over a specific period

questions.correctAnswer: i) To provide a snapshot of a company's financial position at a specific point in time ii) To show a company's revenues and expenses over a specific period

questions.text: Indicate what a debit entry represents in: i) An expense account; (1 mark) ii) A liability account; (1 mark) iii) An asset account; (1 mark) iv) An income account; (1 mark)
questions.correctAnswer: i) An increase in expenses ii) A decrease in liabilities iii) An increase in assets iv) A decrease in income

questions.text: The following account balances were extracted from the books of Linett at the end of her financial year 30 September 2012: ...
questions.correctAnswer: See spreadsheetModelAnswer for the completed Income Statement and Balance Sheet

sections.name: B
sections.title: SECTION B
sections.description: Attempt three of the four questions in this section

questions.text: The bank balance as shown by the cash book of the business is rarely the same as the balance as shown by the bank statement. State and explain the four causes of difference between the two balances that need to be dealt with in the bank reconciliation statement ...
questions.correctAnswer: The four causes of difference are: 1) Cheques not yet presented, 2) Cheques dishonored, 3) Bank charges, and 4) Errors in the cash book or bank statement

questions.text: The cash book of John showed a credit balance of Frw 30,850 on 30 September 2012, whereas the bank statement showed a debit balance of Frw 30,250 on the same date ...
questions.passage: Cheque No. 251 for Frw 4,000 was paid to a supplier on 28 September 2012 but was not presented to the bank until 10 October 2012 ...
questions.correctAnswer: See spreadsheetModelAnswer for the completed Adjusted Cash Book and Bank Reconciliation Statement

questions.text: The following list of balances as at 30 September 2012 has been extracted from the books of Ocampo and Hague, trading partnership ...
questions.passage:
| Account | Frw |
| --- | --- |
| Printing, stationery and postage | 3,500 |
| Sales | 322,100 |
| ... | ... |
questions.correctAnswer: See spreadsheetModelAnswer for the completed Income Statement and Statement of Financial Position

questions.text: The balance sheet of Joab Utaka, a sole trader, as at 31 March 2012 was as follows: ...
questions.passage:
| Account | Frw | Frw |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Capital |  | 1,890 |
| Land and buildings | 1,650 |  |
| ... | ... | ... |
questions.correctAnswer: See spreadsheetModelAnswer for the completed Balance Sheet
